name: α-U thermal stability limit
note: α-U is the low-temperature phase of uranium stable up to 935 K

name: STGB A1 crystallographic plane
note: Type A STGB, tilt axis ⟨100⟩, shear plane (001), low energy

name: STGB A2 crystallographic plane
note: Type A STGB, tilt axis ⟨100⟩, shear plane (001), high energy

name: STGB B1 crystallographic plane
note: Type B STGB, tilt axis ⟨001⟩, shear plane (010), low energy

name: STGB B2 crystallographic plane
note: Type B STGB, tilt axis ⟨001⟩, shear plane (010), high energy

name: STGB C1 crystallographic plane
note: Type C STGB, tilt axis ⟨001⟩, shear plane (100), low energy

name: STGB C2 crystallographic plane
note: Type C STGB, tilt axis ⟨001⟩, shear plane (100), high energy

name: GB energy range at 300 K
note: Egb of the six studied STGBs in α-U at 300 K, from Mahbuba et al. [9]

name: Temperature range for point defect-GB interaction study
note: Temperature range for point defect interaction with GBs

name: Temperature range for GB self-diffusion study
note: Temperature range for GB self-diffusion calculations

name: Weighted average vacancy segregation energy
note: Egb-weighted average vacancy segregation energy for α-U STGBs; not strongly dependent on temperature or GB type

name: Weighted average interstitial segregation energy
note: Egb-weighted average interstitial segregation energy for α-U STGBs; interstitials have greater tendency to segregate than vacancies

name: Maximum vacancy segregation energy
note: Maximum E_s for vacancy observed at A2 GB at 600 K

name: Maximum interstitial segregation energy
note: Maximum E_s for interstitial observed at C2 GB at 500 K

name: Segregation energy increase rate with Egb for interstitials
note: Linear fit: E_s increases with Egb at 2.99 eV per J/m² for interstitials

name: Segregation energy increase rate with Egb for vacancies
note: Linear fit: E_s increases with Egb at 2.92 eV per J/m² for vacancies

name: Egb threshold for vacancy segregation
note: Threshold GB energy above which vacancy segregation becomes favorable

name: A2/A1 interstitial Es ratio at 500 K
note: At 500 K, E_s for interstitial is 7.7 times higher for A2 GB than A1 GB

name: C2/C1 interstitial Es ratio at 500 K
note: At 500 K, E_s for interstitial is 6.2 times higher for C2 GB than C1 GB

name: Weighted average interstitial interaction length
note: Egb-weighted average interaction length for interstitials; double to 11.78 Å for full-length models

name: Weighted average vacancy interaction length
note: Egb-weighted average interaction length for vacancies; double to 6.44 Å for full-length models

name: Maximum interaction length
note: Maximum l_int observed for B1 GB at 600 K

name: GB spatial bin thickness range
note: GB spatial bin thickness related to GB structure from common neighbor analysis

name: GB width for Coble creep
note: GB width observed between 1.5-2.0 nm; 1.6 nm used in Coble creep equation

name: GB area range in diffusion simulations
note: GB area varies between 700-1300 Å² depending on GB type

name: Interstitial defect volume
note: Temperature-averaged interstitial defect volume from Wang et al.

name: Vacancy defect volume
note: Temperature-averaged vacancy defect volume from Wang et al.

note: Highest E_m observed; Table A1

note: Table A1; A1 does not have GB-only diffusion (low energy GB)

note: Table A4

name: Coble creep pre-factor type A
note: Table A4; ε̇_Coble = A_cr × (σ/(T·d³)) × exp(-E_cr/(k_BT))

name: Coble creep activation energy type B
note: Table A4

name: Coble creep pre-factor type B
note: Table A4

name: Coble creep activation energy type C
note: Table A4

name: Coble creep pre-factor type C
note: Table A4

name: Coble creep rate equation pre-exponential for α-U
note: Eq. 11: ε̇_Coble = 8.65×10⁻²³ × (σ/(T·d³)) × exp(-0.215/(k_BT)); σ in MPa, T in K, d in nm

name: Coble creep rate equation activation energy for α-U
note: Eq. 11: overall Coble creep activation energy for α-U

name: Coble creep rate at reactor conditions (small grain)
note: At T=500-750 K, σ=5 MPa, d=0.55 μm (TEM irradiated sample); extremely high creep rate

name: Coble creep rate at reactor conditions (typical grain)
note: At T=500-750 K, σ=5 MPa, d=10-20 μm (typical grain size in metallic UZr fuel)

name: GB-to-bulk diffusion ratio at 750 K (low-energy GBs)
note: GB diffusion ~7×10⁷ times bulk self-diffusion at 750 K for low-energy GBs

name: GB-to-bulk diffusion ratio at 750 K (all GBs)
note: GB diffusion ~8×10¹¹ times bulk self-diffusion at 750 K considering equal probability of all GB types

name: GB diffusion-to-bulk ratio range
note: GB self-diffusion is 10⁷ to 10¹³ times faster than bulk self-diffusion

name: High-energy GB to low-energy GB diffusivity ratio
note: High-energy GBs show approximately 4 times faster diffusivity than low-energy GBs

name: C2/A2 diffusivity ratio at <500 K
note: C2 GB diffusion is 5.5 times higher than A2 GB at temperatures below 500 K

name: C2/B2 diffusivity ratio at <500 K
note: C2 GB diffusion is 8.4 times higher than B2 GB at temperatures below 500 K

name: Interstitial Es ratio at A2 vs A1 (500 K)
note: High-energy GBs have on average 2.5 times higher E_s for interstitials than low-energy GBs

name: Vacancy Es ratio at high vs low energy GBs
note: Vacancy E_s in high-energy GBs is approximately 3 times that of low-energy GBs

name: Self-diffusion enhancement by interstitial addition
note: Inserting an interstitial within the GB interaction zone increases self-diffusion by average 1.5 times

name: Self-diffusion enhancement by vacancy addition
note: Inserting a vacancy increases diffusivity by average 1.45 times for temperatures >600 K

name: α-U α-tearing temperature range
note: Temperature range where jagged voids form along GBs (α-tearing), causing >350% volume increase

name: α-U α-tearing volume increase
note: α-U shows rapid macroscopic swelling (more than 350% volume increase) when α-tearing takes place

name: α-U GB phase transformation onset temperature
note: Above 750 K, ADP potential predicts GBs induce phase transformation to BCC; constrains max study temperature

name: C1 GB+V to GB+I diffusivity ratio
note: C1 GB maintains approximately constant ratio of 2.3 between GB+V and GB+I conditions

name: Bulk interstitial migration energy range
note: Prior MD and DFT studies: bulk interstitial E_m = 0.2-0.4 eV

name: Bulk vacancy migration energy range
note: Prior MD and DFT studies: bulk vacancy E_m = 0.3-0.4 eV

name: GB migration energy range
note: GB migration energy varies from 0.1 to 0.6 eV across all studied GBs and conditions

name: Type B/C to type A Coble creep ratio at 750 K
note: At 750 K, type B has 1.4 times higher diffusional creep rate than type A

name: Type C to type A Coble creep ratio at 750 K
note: At 750 K, type C has 2.4 times higher diffusional creep rate than type A

name: Metallic fuel operating temperature range
note: Prototypical metallic fuel operation conditions temperature range

name: Metallic fuel gas/plenum pressure
note: Gas/plenum pressure used for Coble creep calculation at reactor conditions

name: Metallic fuel average grain size (irradiated TEM)
note: Average grain size from TEM analysis of irradiated sample C from Di Lemma

name: Typical grain size in metallic UZr fuel
note: Typical grain size in metallic UZr fuel from Metallic Fuels Handbook

name: Coble creep activation energy comparison with UO₂
note: UO₂ Coble creep activation energy is ~18× higher than α-U (0.221 eV)

name: α-U elastic constant [010] reduction fraction
note: Elastic constant in [010] direction is 30% smaller compared to the other two directions

name: Self-diffusion anisotropy in α-U at 900 K
note: Self-diffusion in [100] and [001] directions is ~20 times higher than [010] at 900 K

name: Segregation energy ratio interstitial/vacancy at B1 (500 K)
note: For low-energy GBs, interstitial E_s is approximately 6 times vacancy E_s at B1 GB

name: A1 interstitial interaction length range
note: A1 GB interstitial l_int ranges between 2.9-7.7 Å; one order lower than other GBs

name: C1 interstitial interaction length range
note: C1 GB interstitial l_int varies between 6.1-9.0 Å

name: Simulation runtime for diffusion
note: NVT run time 3-5.5 ns; higher temperatures use shorter relaxation times

name: Nosé-Hoover barostat damping factor
note: Damping factor for anisotropic relaxation in all directions
